humanSummary.text: KAIFUU-053 delivers the capability-leveled engine detector registry as specified. `kaifuu_core::registry::capability` ships `CapabilityLevel` (Identify < Inventory < Extract < Patch, `Ord` ladder confirmed at `capability.rs:22`), `CapabilityLevelStatus` (Supported / Partial { limitations } / Unsupported { reason }), and `AdapterCapabilityMatrix` with the per-rung typed status. `kaifuu_core::registry::AdapterRegistry::adapters_supporting(level)` is strict-Supported-only (`registry/mod.rs:30-36`) - test `adapters_supporting_extract_excludes_identify_and_inventory_only_adapters` (`registry/mod.rs:152-157`) asserts the identify-only adapter is excluded from `adapters_supporting(Extract)`; test `adapters_supporting_partial_extract_still_excludes_them` (`registry/mod.rs:175-197`) asserts that even Partial-at-Extract is excluded. All four acceptance criteria are satisfied. (1) Typed enum `CapabilityLevel` and `CapabilityLevelStatus` are first-class Rust types, not strings; the kebab/snake-case wire form is pinned via `#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]` and the `as_str` helper (`capability.rs:38-46`). (2) Identify-only adapters never appear in `adapters_supporting(Extract)` or `adapters_supporting(Patch)`: the Siglus detector at `kaifuu-engine-fixture/src/lib.rs:3137` uses `AdapterCapabilityMatrix::identify_only(...)` which yields `Supported(Identify)` + three `Unsupported` rungs; the test above directly exercises this contract. (3) TS + Rust + DB parity: TS adds `assertCapabilityLevelStatusV02` (`packages/localization-bridge-schema/src/index.ts:3263-3302`) and `assertAdapterCapabilityMatrixV02` (`index.ts:3307-3315`), both hand-written - no Zod import; `grep -n "zod\|Zod" packages/localization-bridge-schema/src/index.ts` returns zero hits in this slice. The DB migration `0028_engine_capability_reports.sql` declares two Postgres enums (`capability_level_enum`, `capability_level_status_kind`) and a CHECK constraint that mirrors the Rust discriminator: `supported -> no reason, no limitations`; `partial -> reason null, limitations is a non-empty jsonb array`; `unsupported -> reason non-empty, limitations empty`. (4) Existing detector migration without breaking output: the four detectors (`FIXTURE_ADAPTER_ID`, `XP3_DETECTOR_ADAPTER_ID`, `SIGLUS_DETECTOR_ADAPTER_ID`, `REALLIVE_DETECTOR_ADAPTER_ID`) each declare their `level_matrix` via `with_level_matrix(...)`, including a clean-room provenance preamble for the RealLive detector (`kaifuu-engine-fixture/src/lib.rs:1-19`). Itotori consumer plumbing lands cleanly: `apps/itotori/src/services/engine-capability-report.ts` carries `EngineCapabilityReportPort`, the repository (`packages/itotori-db/src/repositories/engine-capability-report-repository.ts`, 248 LOC), the dashboard panel (`apps/itotori/src/dashboard.ts`, 97 added LOC), and two CLI commands. The kaifuu-engine-playbook update at `docs/kaifuu-engine-playbook.md:223-264` documents the ladder rules every adapter author follows and pins the next-rung policy as a coordinated Rust+TS+DB bump. The `derive_from_reports` mapping (mentioned in the playbook) is a safety net; the explicit `with_level_matrix` declaration is the contract. Verification reruns: `cargo test -p kaifuu-core` 190 passed / 0 failed; full workspace test result spans across `kaifuu-core` (190), `kaifuu-cli` (78), `kaifuu-engine-fixture` (22), `kaifuu-reallive` (22+11+11+9+32+8+5+3+5+3), `utsushi-core` (611+7+10+9+9+14+3+5+15+11), totalling ~1100+ tests passed / 0 failed / 5 ignored; `pnpm exec vp run ts:test` reports 207 passed + 3 skipped across 17 test files; `just check` clean (cargo fmt, schema:check, ts:typecheck, cargo check --workspace). DB integration test (`pnpm --filter @itotori/db test:db`) not run in this audit (requires `just db-up`); the in-source TS + SQL CHECK constraints pin every invariant the integration test would re-verify. Two P3 follow-ups: (F001) migration is numbered `0028` and so are UTSUSHI-030 and ITOTORI-013's migrations - orchestrator must renumber two at merge; (F002) the playbook update names `derive_from_reports` as a safety-net mapping but the source code declares `with_level_matrix` is preferred; the safety-net is only invoked when an adapter omits the explicit declaration - in this slice every detector ships an explicit declaration, so the safety net is currently dead code. Document the policy or add a `#[deny(missing_level_matrix)]`-style fence so future adapters cannot silently rely on the fallback.

findings.title: Migration number 0028 collides with sibling UTSUSHI-030 and ITOTORI-013
findings.description: KAIFUU-053, UTSUSHI-030, and ITOTORI-013 each ship a migration named `0028_*.sql`. Each is internally consistent on its branch (no overlap in tables; `_engine_capability_reports`, `_runtime_conformance_results`, `_scene_summaries` are disjoint, and the two Postgres enums KAIFUU-053 creates are also disjoint). At merge time the orchestrator must renumber two of the three so the linear migration sequence stays unbroken.

evidence.detail: ls packages/itotori-db/migrations/ | tail -5 on each worktree shows three concurrent 0028_* migrations.

findings.impact: On merge, `pnpm --filter @itotori/db test:db` will refuse the duplicate migration id. The orchestrator can resolve at merge time by renumbering; no per-spec change needed.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator renames two of the three 0028_* migrations to 0029_* / 0030_* during merge and updates `packages/itotori-db/src/migrations.ts` accordingly.

acceptanceCriteria: At merge into main, renumber the migration so it does not collide with UTSUSHI-030 / ITOTORI-013; update `migrations.ts`.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: This is a merge-time orchestrator task, not an in-branch fix.

findings.title: derive_from_reports safety-net invites silent fallback for future detectors
findings.description: The playbook update at `docs/kaifuu-engine-playbook.md:234-237` describes `derive_from_reports` as a "conservative safety net" for adapters that omit `with_level_matrix`. In this slice every detector ships an explicit declaration, so the safety net is currently dead code. A future detector author who omits the declaration will silently get the derived mapping, which may over- or under-claim against the per-rung intent. The playbook documents the rule but the type system does not enforce it.

evidence.detail: kaifuu-engine-playbook.md:234-237 - "Declare `level_matrix` explicitly via `AdapterCapabilities::with_level_matrix`. The default `derive_from_reports` mapping is conservative and is a safety net, not a substitute for the explicit declaration."

findings.impact: A new detector that forgets `with_level_matrix` will compile and ship a derived matrix that may not match the author's intent. The strict-Supported gate in `adapters_supporting` could then either drop the adapter from `Extract` consumers or promote it incorrectly.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Either remove `derive_from_reports` as a fallback and require `with_level_matrix` at construction, or add a `debug_assert!` / lint-style fence that warns when the fallback is invoked, with documented test coverage.

proposedDagNode.title: Force explicit level_matrix declaration on every adapter
proposedDagNode.summary: Make `with_level_matrix` mandatory at construction time so the `derive_from_reports` fallback cannot silently produce a wrong mapping. Either move the matrix into `AdapterCapabilities::new` or add a `debug_assert!` that fires when an adapter ships without an explicit declaration.
deliverables: Test that asserts every adapter in `kaifuu-engine-fixture` ships an explicit declaration.
Playbook updated to drop the safety-net language.
acceptanceCriteria: An adapter that does not call `with_level_matrix` fails to compile or fails a debug-assert at construction time.
Every existing detector continues to register without behavioural drift in `adapters_supporting(...)`.
auditFocus: Adapter authors get a compile- or test-time failure if they forget the declaration.
